sections.title: Ship your Godot game to iOS — no Mac required.

inlineContent.text: Learn how to set up ShipGodot for automatic export, signing, and publishing of your Godot game to TestFlight & App Store in a single push.
No Xcode headaches. No certificate nightmares. Just follow the steps.

metadata.title: Ship your Godot game to iOS — no Mac required.

doc://ShipGodotIOS/tutorials/table-of-co.title: Ship your Godot game to iOS — no Mac required.

abstract.text: Learn how to set up ShipGodot for automatic export, signing, and publishing of your Godot game to TestFlight & App Store in a single push.
No Xcode headaches. No certificate nightmares. Just follow the steps.
In this chapter, you’ll gather and securely store various secrets—like API keys and deploy keys—that will be reused across all future projects using ShipGodot iOS.

doc://ShipGodotIOS/tutorials/ShipGodotIO.title: Shipping Your Game to the App Store

abstract.text: In this chapter, you’ll go through the full cycle of deploying updates to your testers, and when the time feels right, publishing your game to the App Store for the world to play.
In this section, you’ll prepare everything required to get started with ShipGodot.
Once these steps are completed, you’ll be fully prepared to use the project and begin building your dream iOS game.
In this chapter, we’ll walk through the process of configuring your Godot project for the ShipGodot pipeline,
setting up a deployment repository from the provided template, and connecting the two together.
To guide you through the process, we’ll be using the Multitouch Cubes demo from Godot’s official demo projects as our example.
In this chapter, you’ll go through everything needed to get your first build live on TestFlight.
You’ll create your app on App Store Connect, configure all the required secrets in your deployment repository,
generate the necessary certificates and provisioning profiles, and finally trigger the pipeline to build and upload your game automatically.
